description: No-key coding-agent repo memory retrieval benchmark.

records.query: What command should the next agent run for Python tests?

cards.summary: Run the Python test suite with .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q.
cards.citation: README.md lines 28-33 | "Run the Python test suite with .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q."

cards.summary: Before handing off to another coding agent, run openbook handoff so the next agent gets a compact context pack instead of rereading all project docs.
cards.citation: docs/slash-commands.md lines 18-30 | "Before handing off to another coding agent, run openbook handoff so the next agent gets a compact context pack instead of rereading all project docs."

cards.summary: MCP installs must set OPENBOOK_PROJECT so each client opens the correct repo memory book even when launched from another working directory.
cards.citation: docs/mcp.md lines 5-12 | "MCP installs must set OPENBOOK_PROJECT so each client opens the correct repo memory book even when launched from another working directory."

records.query: Is SQLite or Markdown the source of truth for OpenBook memory?

cards.summary: SQLite is the source of truth for OpenBook memory. Markdown and JSON files are exports, not the primary memory store.
cards.citation: docs/architecture.md lines 12-20 | "SQLite is the source of truth for OpenBook memory. Markdown and JSON files are exports, not the primary memory store."

cards.summary: The default OpenBook path requires no Docker, no external vector database, and no model provider account; SQLite FTS works with no API key.
cards.citation: docs/installation.md lines 3-8 | "The default OpenBook path requires no Docker, no external vector database, and no model provider account; SQLite FTS works with no API key."

cards.summary: Before handing off to another coding agent, run openbook handoff so the next agent gets a compact context pack instead of rereading all project docs.
cards.citation: docs/slash-commands.md lines 18-30 | "Before handing off to another coding agent, run openbook handoff so the next agent gets a compact context pack instead of rereading all project docs."

records.query: Why should MCP config pin the project path?

cards.summary: MCP installs must set OPENBOOK_PROJECT so each client opens the correct repo memory book even when launched from another working directory.

cards.summary: The default OpenBook path requires no Docker, no external vector database, and no model provider account; SQLite FTS works with no API key.

cards.summary: A marketing landing page can use large hero imagery, but operational coding tools should prioritize compact task surfaces.
cards.citation: AGENTS.md lines 60-70 | "A marketing landing page can use large hero imagery, but operational coding tools should prioritize compact task surfaces."

records.query: How should operational frontend task surfaces be styled?

cards.summary: Frontend task surfaces should be dense, restrained, and workflow-focused; do not turn operational tools into marketing landing pages.
cards.citation: AGENTS.md lines 40-55 | "Frontend task surfaces should be dense, restrained, and workflow-focused; do not turn operational tools into marketing landing pages."

cards.summary: A marketing landing page can use large hero imagery, but operational coding tools should prioritize compact task surfaces.

cards.summary: Clean public benchmark reports live under benchmarks/published; scratch runs under benchmarks/**/results are ignored.
cards.citation: benchmarks/README.md lines 10-18 | "Clean public benchmark reports live under benchmarks/published; scratch runs under benchmarks/**/results are ignored."

records.query: What should I do if a long provider benchmark times out?

cards.summary: Long provider-backed benchmarks can hit read timeouts; rerun with the same report directory because checkpoint.records.jsonl resumes completed items.
cards.citation: benchmarks/README.md lines 52-70 | "Long provider-backed benchmarks can hit read timeouts; rerun with the same report directory because checkpoint.records.jsonl resumes completed items."

cards.summary: Gemini provider examples use gemini-embedding-2 for embeddings, gemini-3-flash-preview for the reader, and gemini-3.1-pro-preview for judging.
